pr.title: Better error for unknown func/TU in decomp.py
pr.author: thefoxcam

summary: Updated tools/decomp.py to produce a clearer error when decomp.py cannot resolve the user input as either a function name or a translation unit asm file. Instead of only reporting a missing asm path, the script now explicitly says no function named by the input was found and separately reports the expected asm file path for the TU case.
what_changed: In tools/decomp.py, the fallback error path in main() was changed from one generic missing-asm-file message to two more specific stderr messages.
The new first message covers the function-name interpretation: `If a function was intended, then no function with the name <{m2c_input}> was found.`
The new second message covers the translation-unit interpretation: `If a TU was intended, then the expected asm file does not exist at path {asm_file}`.
The script still exits with status 1 after printing the error.
smart_moves: Preserved the existing failure behavior while improving diagnosis, minimizing risk to the decomp tooling flow.
Separated the two possible user intents, function lookup versus TU asm path lookup, so a failed invocation tells the user which assumptions were tried.
Included both the original user-provided m2c input and the computed asm file path, making the error actionable for typos and missing-file cases.
decomp_lessons: Decomp helper scripts should make ambiguous inputs explicit in errors, especially when a single argument can mean either a function name or a translation unit.
When tooling performs fallback resolution, report each failed resolution path rather than only the last one.
Small developer-experience fixes in scripts can reduce confusion during matching work without touching game code or affecting binary matching.
naming_conventions: The tool-facing message uses `TU` as shorthand for translation unit.
The function-name error displays the searched function name inside angle brackets, matching the new format `<{m2c_input}>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic changed directly; this PR only improved diagnostics around locating the function or asm file used by the decompilation helper.
The improved error helps identify whether the matching workflow failed because the function symbol was unknown or because the expected asm file for a TU was absent.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only recorded issue-comment count was 1, but no human text excerpt was provided, so no specific reviewer intent can be extracted.
searchable_terms: Better error for unknown func/TU in decomp.py
follow_up_queries: Find other decomp.py error paths that conflate function-name lookup with TU asm-file lookup.
Search for tooling errors that only report computed file paths but not the original user input.
Look for docs or usage examples of tools/decomp.py that should mention function versus TU input behavior.
